title: Threat Intel: new zero-day exploit discovered 2026
content:
MCP issues detected. Run /mcp list for status.I will search for information regarding any new zero-day exploits discovered in 2026.
The top 5 zero-day exploits and significant security incidents discovered in early 2026 are:

1.  **Google Chrome: CVE-2026-5281 (April 2026)**
    *   **Description:** A high-severity "use-after-free" bug in **Dawn** (the WebGPU implementation for Chromium). It allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary code via a malicious HTML page.
    *   **URL:** [csoonline.com](https://www.csoonline.com/article/3215843/google-patches-fourth-chrome-zero-day-of-2026.html)

2.  **TrueConf: CVE-2026-3502 (April 2026)**
    *   **Description:** A vulnerability in the updater validation of the TrueConf videoconferencing client. It has been used by Chinese-nexus threat actors to target government entities in Southeast Asia by pushing malicious updates.
    *   **URL:** [helpnetsecurity.com](https://www.helpnetsecurity.com/2026/04/03/trueconf-zero-day-exploited/)

3.  **C
